title: High Protein Smoothie Bowl
metaDescription: Thick, spoonable high protein smoothie bowl with 26g of protein per serving — frozen berries, Greek yogurt, and cottage cheese blended smooth. Ready in 5 minutes.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: protein smoothie bowl, high protein smoothie, breakfast bowl
ingredients: 1/4 cup milk (dairy or unsweetened almond milk)
Toppings: sliced banana, granola, chia seeds, fresh raspberries

steps.name: Blend the base
steps.text: Add frozen berries, cottage cheese, Greek yogurt, milk, honey, and frozen banana to a high-speed blender.

steps.name: Blend until thick
steps.text: Blend on high, stopping to scrape down the sides, until completely smooth and thick — it should hold its shape on a spoon, not pour like a drink.

steps.name: Pour into a bowl
steps.text: Pour the thick smoothie into a chilled bowl using a spatula to help it along.

steps.name: Top and serve
steps.text: Arrange banana slices, granola, chia seeds, and fresh raspberries on top. Serve immediately with a spoon.

tips.question: Why is my smoothie bowl too thin?
tips.answer: Not enough frozen fruit, or the blender ran too long and melted it. Use fully frozen berries and banana, and blend in short pulses rather than continuously.

tips.question: Can I make this without cottage cheese?
tips.answer: Yes — swap it for an extra 1/2 cup of Greek yogurt, though the protein content will drop slightly and the texture will be a bit less thick.

tips.question: Can I prep the base ahead of time?
tips.answer: Blend and freeze the base (without toppings) in a flat container for up to 1 month. Let it soften 5-10 minutes before topping and serving.

tips.question: What blender works best for thick smoothie bowls?
tips.answer: A high-speed blender with a tamper works best since the mixture is much thicker than a drinkable smoothie — a low-power blender may struggle without added liquid, which thins the texture.
